title: Quantum Kaworu
wikitext:
[]: Quantum Kaworu?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:32 pm I though this could live or die as a new topic... Giji Shinka wrote: Kaworu might be a looper within rebuilt universe but nothing else. (There has been many implication to that direction, and i hope that Kaworu would remain as mysterious as he was in 1.0, 2.0, 3.0. And i don't need any more explanations about him)

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:51 pm Kaworu looping within just the rebuild timeline could make sense. Dies in Eva 13 after failing to make Shinji happy ---> Wakes up in the next coffin on the moon in front of the Mark 6 ---> "This time, I'll show you true happiness " --> repeat. But surely Kaworu wouldn't keep falling for the same gambit if he is stuck in some kind of loop.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:53 pm @Pwhodges Could be. Although, it makes me wonder why "Quantum" Rei appears out of nowhere and disappears, doesn't say anything or won't communicate. (Well, one explanation for the part why she won't say anything could be that she's.....Well....Rei) "Quantum" Kaworu on the other hand doesn't disappear and he communicates with other people....

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Tue Jul 09, 2013 2:05 pm It's an interesting idea. But I have to wonder if in fact he has gone 'quantum', he could've and would've used his quantum powers during the Fourth Impact. It doesn't make sense for Kaoru to fail and die without achieving his goal, Shinji's happiness, if he had the quantum powers and was more 'vocal'. I do agree to some extent that he may be a looper(strictly within the rebuild universe) or something close to that effect which allows him to come back again and again. There is also that CRC explanation about the coffins on the moon. So there is also the possibility that Kaoru may simply be like series of clones like that of Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Jul 09, 2013 2:08 pm If Kaworu possesses the full scope of powers a seemingly godlike Quantum Rei possesses I don't see why he'd need to loop. If he fucks up at one point it's just a matter of going back a tiny bit and changing a variable, but he seemingly can't do that. He wakes up on the moon and he sits there for weeks (months?) before making his move. From what I can tell he seems less a god and more an entity stuck in the loop until something is achieved, be it the outcome he desires, another outcome, or it's done a specific amount of times. I think he's more Sam Beckett and less God.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 09, 2013 2:44 pm If Kaworu really was doing this for the second time, then why would he be that shocked about/fall for such a plan?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 09, 2013 3:00 pm TruthfulLie wrote: I have to wonder if in fact he has gone 'quantum', he could've and would've used his quantum powers during the Fourth Impact. It doesn't make sense for Kaoru to fail and die without achieving his goal, Shinji's happiness, if he had the quantum powers and was more 'vocal'.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 09, 2013 3:10 pm pwhodges wrote: His quantum over-view may have shown him that behaving like that is in fact the path that leads to Shinji's greatest happiness by the end of Final (even if we can't yet see that). (...) He sees all, knows all, from his quantum over-view, and thus behaves with the inscrutability of someone who knows things that we don't.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jul 09, 2013 3:29 pm It's perfectly clear that there's multiple Kaworus involved. Look at the 2.0 design (henceforth refered to as Haworu) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Tue Jul 09, 2013 4:09 pm I still say it's a "Higurashi no Naku Koro ni" situation, with Kwarou as Rika. His VA seems to agree.

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Tue Jul 09, 2013 4:11 pm pwhodges wrote: His quantum over-view may have shown him that behaving like that is in fact the path that leads to Shinji's greatest happiness by the end of Final (even if we can't yet see that).

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Jul 09, 2013 6:37 pm Kendrix wrote: If Kaworu really was doing this for the second time, then why would he be that shocked about/fall for such a plan?

[]:             Quote Post by moonwolf2024 » Tue Jul 09, 2013 10:11 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: It's perfectly clear that there's multiple Kaworus involved. Look at the 2.0 design (henceforth refered to as Haworu) SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Jul 09, 2013 10:25 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Ignoring the obvious fact that designs changed between movies in the past and have changed again it is only reasonable to conclude these are two seperate beings. Haworu has broader shoulders while Qworu has a shaplier butt. The two of them also differ in the proportion of body to leg length. Haworu is much closer looking to Sadamoto's style and given his ominously ambiguous remarks in 1.0 and 2.0 might be just as much of a bastard. Qworu on the other hand has a much more pleasant face complection and got a haircut almost in the style of Kabuto Kouji sans sideburns. The question remains which is the true Kaworu of the rebuild continuity and which is the imposter? Which one will appear to Shinji in Final? Or are they all working together? Is Ishida full of it with his comments? What if Masayuki's creepy Death Kaworu makes an apperance? Will I end up having to apologize if this silly post ruins the thread?

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Tue Jul 09, 2013 11:06 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Ignoring the obvious fact that designs changed between movies in the past and have changed again it is only reasonable to conclude these are two seperate beings. Haworu has broader shoulders while Qworu has a shaplier butt. The two of them also differ in the proportion of body to leg length. Haworu is much closer looking to Sadamoto's style and given his ominously ambiguous remarks in 1.0 and 2.0 might be just as much of a bastard. Qworu on the other hand has a much more pleasant face complection and got a haircut almost in the style of Kabuto Kouji sans sideburns.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jul 09, 2013 11:49 pm Masayuki is one of the co-directors on Rebuild and was an animation director, episode directors, etc during the NGE days. When they made Death and needed to fix some scene to bring them up to movie quality he was the one that redrew smiling Rei post-Remiel battle in an overly detailed and creepy way. He was also the one that did the layouts for the Terminal Dogma fight in ep 24 and for Death gave Kaworu the same occasional creepy fix. If you look back at NGE era artwork and see an unusual amount of detail in the hair and very pointy faces it's usually Masayuki's doing. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by cloudxsolder » Tue Jul 09, 2013 11:50 pm I'm starting to agree it's possible that the Kawuro we see in 1.0 + 2.0 aren't the same kawuro as 3.0 And everything he says in the first 2 films seems to suggest he knows exactly what went on in 3.0 Having a little fun here, mostly fanwankery but I'll share regardless A theory on how he never sees it coming: What if at some point after 3I (or during, or before for that matter) He died. Another clone is then made, not by Seele, but by Gendo, only kawuro THINKS he was made by Seele: After Seele "Freeze", Gendo comes to possess all the tools and information they had to create a Kawuro. This kawuro is made and altered in such a way that his memory of the 14yr skip is altered, and therefore can be used as a tool to help Gendo. So he plunges into Dogma with Shinji and has his revelation, but it's to late. He has no choice but to die in order to save Shinji. He dies and then boom, he goes back to the end of 1.0 with his mixed memories from from 3.0 and prior. This explains his "This time, I will definitely make you happy" AND it also explains his reference to Gendo in 2.0 as "father" Let's remember now, when seele first made him, they had no reason to wipe his memory of anything, or alter it in anyway, because nothing had happened yet. This Kawuro (we see in 1.0) also doesn't have enough information about what ACTUALLY happened around 3I to avoid his demise because his memory of the event was altered in some way. He may very well have a memory of what happened, but it isn't correct (and he knows this). As godly as kawuro is, having adam's soul and all - The SDSS still trump ADAM. Adam was created by the FIR and the SDSS where created as an instruction manual. Seele (and Gendo no doubt in 3.0) have the knowledge to 'bend him to their will' So yes, in a way, Kaworu is stuck in a loop. By the hands of Gendo and got his awakened EVA at the same time in order to power his ship. So, from here, he goes back to the end of 1.0 and does his best to stay alive and help Shinji but his memory of how he actually died during the 14 year time skip has been altered. He cannot avoid it. :P

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jul 10, 2013 12:34 am Hmm. I created this thread to try out a theory which might resolve some of the conundrums about Kaworu without invoking looping (which was widely considered unacceptable); but now it seems to be turning into a stronghold of making that very looping more respectable!

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 10, 2013 2:43 am I'm not even sure whether to split out all the looper and multi-Kaworu stuff, because it's technically off-topic. This topic is about an entirely new perspective, of an all-seeing quantum Kaworu who's doing the full Casablanca business. Several posts don't even address the topic at all and just launch into "Well I think there's actually two Kaworus" and such. Either that or the topic can be expanded to just be the new home thread for all discussion of Kaworu's potential time-surpassing nature.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jul 10, 2013 3:08 am I would prefer that you take the second option : just change the title to make the topic encompass all the theories about Kaworu's strange knowledge("this time") and supposed Time Lord powers, too much little topics risks to dilute the debate.
summary:
[]: Quantum Kaworu?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 09, 2013 1:32 pm I though this could live or die as a new topic... Giji Shinka wrote: Kaworu might be a looper within rebuilt universe but nothing else. (There has been many implication to that direction, and i hope that Kaworu would remain as mysterious as he was in 1.0, 2.0, 3.0. And i don't need any more explanations about him)

Article: Quantum Kaworu
2.0 aren't the same kawuro as 3.0 And everything he says in the first 2 films seems to suggest he knows exactly what went on in 3.0 Having a little fun here, mostly fanwankery but I'll share regardless A theory on how he never sees it coming: What if at some point after 3I (or during, or before for that matter) He died. Another clone is then made, not by Seele, but by Gendo, only kawuro THINKS he was made by Seele: After Seele "Freeze", Gendo comes to possess all the tools and information they had to create a Kawuro. This kawuro is made and altered in such a way that his memory of the 14yr skip is altered, and therefore can be used as a tool to help Gendo. So he plunges into Dogma with Shinji and has his revelation, but it's to late. He has no choice but to die in order to save Shinji. He dies and then boom, he goes back to the end of 1.0 with his mixed memories from from 3.0 and prior. This explains his "This time, I will definitely make you happy" AND it also explains his reference to Gendo in 2.0 as "father" Let's remember now, when seele first made him, they had no reason to wipe his memory of anything, or alter it in anyway, because nothing had happened yet. This Kawuro (we see in 1.0) also doesn't have enough information about what ACTUALLY happened around 3I to avoid his demise because his memory of the event was altered in some way. He may very well have a memory of what happened, but it isn't correct (and he knows this). As godly as kawuro is, having adam's soul and all - The SDSS still trump ADAM. Adam was created by the FIR and the SDSS where created as an instruction manual. Seele (and Gendo no doubt in 3.0) have the knowledge to 'bend him to their will' So yes, in a way, Kaworu is stuck in a loop. By the hands of Gendo and got his awakened EVA at the same time in order to power his ship. So, from here, he goes back to the end of 1.0 and does his best to stay alive and help Shinji but his memory of

Article: Quantum Kaworu
 Gendo and got his awakened EVA at the same time in order to power his ship. So, from here, he goes back to the end of 1.0 and does his best to stay alive and help Shinji but his memory of how he actually died during the 14 year time skip has been altered. He cannot avoid it. :P
